reference_card_samples.text: C12021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline Slide Set, Cardiovascular emergencies - Chest pain possible ACSHuman verifiedQuote-backedscore 2.044semantic 0lexical 0.629initial_managementsociety_guideline2021View sourceVerify basisCopy quote/search phraseOriginal quoteshould be used routinelyQuote-backed ACS management summary: use a structured pathway that combines symptoms, ECG, serial troponin strategy, risk profile, monitoring needs, and disposition rather than a generic red-flag paragraph.quote_acs_decision_pathway_004

C2Opioid Overdose Reversal Medications, Toxicology and substance use - Naloxone response and recurrenceHuman verifiedQuote-backedscore 1.734semantic 0lexical 0.299initial_managementsociety_guideline2025View sourceVerify basisCopy quote/search phraseOriginal quoteMedical assistance must be obtained as soon as possibleQuote-backed naloxone response management summary: initial improvement should trigger ongoing observation, emergency evaluation, repeat support when needed, and attention to withdrawal, recurrence, and co-ingestants.quote_naloxone_medical_help_009

C3Severe Agitation, Psychiatric and behavioral emergencies - Severe agitation medication strategyHuman verifiedQuote-backedscore 1.661semantic 0lexical 0.196initial_managemented_specific_guideline2023View sourceVerify basisCopy quote/search phraseOriginal quoteconsider ketamine (intravenous or intramuscular) to rapidly treat severe agitationQuote-backed severe agitation management summary: reserve rapid dissociative medication for safety-critical agitation, with airway-ready monitoring, reassessment, and documentation of the safety threat.quote_agitation_ketamine_004

C42021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline Slide Set, Cardiovascular emergencies - Non-ST elevation ACSHuman verifiedQuote-backedscore 1.974semantic 0lexical 0.559initial_managementsociety_guideline2021View sourceVerify basisCopy quote/search phraseOriginal quotetreated according to STEMI and NSTE-ACS guidelinesQuote-backed ACS management summary: abnormal ECG evidence should shift the learner from undifferentiated chest pain to syndrome-specific treatment, monitoring, cardiology escalation, and disposition planning.quote_acs_guideline_pathway_005
